sections.heading: Clinical Note
sections.section_value:
Chief Complaint
Facial pain, specifically stabbing and shooting pain in the right cheekbone area.
History of Present Illness
The patient reports experiencing stabbing, shooting pain in his right cheekbone area for approximately two to three months, with episodes lasting up to an hour. He has tried ibuprofen and tiger balm without relief. He denies any sensory loss or weakness in the affected face or elsewhere. He occasionally experiences headaches, but not migraines. He reports no personal or family history of multiple sclerosis or brain tumors. He takes occasional Tylenol for headaches.
Findings
An MRI revealed a small blood vessel abutting and potentially compressing the trigeminal nerve. The patient reports a pain level of 10 during episodes.
Assessment
The patient likely has trigeminal neuralgia secondary to compression of the trigeminal nerve by a blood vessel.
Plan
We will initiate treatment with tegretol, starting with a low dose, and monitor for side effects such as memory loss, tingling, imbalance, or dermatologic skin reactions. We will also monitor for potential side effects such as memory loss, tingling, or imbalance. Follow-up will be scheduled in four weeks to assess the effectiveness of the medication and to discuss surgical options if necessary.
